# Specification: #208 — Build review-pr high-order play with diff-scoped quality standards, severity taxonomy, and reviewer selection
<!-- Generated by start-feature-planning | Plan sub-agent output -->

**Intent:** Introduce an automated, diff-scoped PR review capability as an high-order play (`review-pr`) in meridian-os, backed by a deterministic severity taxonomy, a confidence gate, and git-history-driven reviewer selection, and integrate it into `ship` as a bypass-conditional pre-merge step.

**Constraints:**
- IDD: `reference/intent.yaml` is source of truth, `SKILL.md` is compiled via `/create-play`. Never hand-edit SKILL.md.
- L2 budget: ≤5 domain agents (utility agents exempt).
- Standards hierarchy KB → LTM → STM, paths resolved from `core/config.yaml` only — no hardcoded paths.
- Every finding MUST cite an existing standard ID (CODE-nn, SEC-nn, ARCH-nn, TEST-nn, DOC-nn, PERF-nn, FE-nn, BE-nn, DATA-nn, OPS-nn, DEBT-nn).
- Severity classification MUST be deterministic — same PR run twice produces identical P1..P4 buckets.
- Diff-scoped execution only — `review-pr` NEVER runs full-repo `quality-check`.
- `ship` must read `review-pr.bypass` and gate merge on quality findings + confidence when bypass is false.
- `create-pr` must NOT be modified; reviewer selection lives only in `review-pr`.

**Failure Conditions:**
- Severity taxonomy uses subjective/LLM-judged rules or does not cite real standard IDs.
- `core/config.yaml` `review-pr` section missing any of: `bypass`, `confidence_threshold`, `reviewer_count`, `standards_order`, `fallback_reviewers`, `activity_window_days`.
- `quality-check-scoped` duplicates `/quality-check` KB or 11-subagent architecture instead of reusing KB data with a single diff-bounded pass.
- `review-pr` agent dispatches exceed L2 domain budget (>5 domain agents).
- `ship` re-bake omitted — `ship` still runs without the review gate when `bypass=false`.
- A finding emitted without a cited standard ID (F3 guard).
- Reviewer selection returns <3 and no `fallback_reviewers` supplied.
- `create-pr` modified or `idsd.md` not updated for P13.
- Intent-hash drift between `ship`/`review-pr` `intent.yaml` and their compiled `SKILL.md`.

## Summary

This work builds the `review-pr` high-order play plus the supporting scaffolding it needs: (1) a deterministic PR severity taxonomy expressed as a mapping from existing KB standard IDs to P1..P4 buckets, (2) a `review-pr` section in `core/config.yaml`, (3) a new `quality-check-scoped` skill that reuses KB data but runs a single-pass diff-bounded evaluation (not the 11-subagent full-repo framework), (4) the compiled `review-pr` play itself with 6 steps (context load → scoped quality eval → confidence → reviewer selection → post & route → evidence), and (5) a delta to `ship` so that when `review-pr.bypass=false` it inserts `review-pr` as Step 2.5 between `create-pr` and `merge-pr` with P1 findings or low confidence halting the pipeline. `create-pr` is intentionally untouched — reviewer-selection logic belongs only to `review-pr`. IDSD is updated to flip P13 to COMPLETE and document the new bypass-conditional ship step.

**Path correction vs the issue body:** The issue places the taxonomy at `core/components/memory/kb/standards/pr-severity-taxonomy.md`, but meridian-os has no `kb/` directory. KB knowledge lives at `core/components/memory/knowledge/` and cross-cutting rules live at `core/components/memory/standards/`. The taxonomy is a standard (a rule set), so the correct path is **`core/components/memory/standards/pr-severity-taxonomy.md`**. The review-pr play resolves this path via `core/config.yaml` (no hardcoding), preserving the spirit of "KB severity taxonomy cited by standard ID."

## Affected Files

| File | Role | Change Needed |
|---|---|---|
| `core/components/memory/standards/pr-severity-taxonomy.md` | New KB standard | CREATE — P1..P4 buckets as mechanical mappings from existing standard IDs with grep-style matchers |
| `core/config.yaml` | Config | MODIFY — add `review-pr:` section and `standards:` resolution block for the taxonomy path |
| `core/components/skills/quality-check-scoped/SKILL.md` | New skill | CREATE via `/skill-creator` — diff-bounded single-pass evaluator, reuses `core/components/memory/knowledge/quality/**` KB |
| `core/components/skills/quality-check-scoped/reference/` | Skill reference | CREATE — contract schema, finding schema, link to taxonomy |
| `core/components/plays/review-pr/reference/intent.yaml` | New play intent | CREATE — constraints C1..C10, failure F1..F9, scenarios S1..S4 |
| `core/components/plays/review-pr/SKILL.md` | Compiled play | CREATE via `/create-play --bake review-pr` (do NOT hand-edit) |
| `core/components/plays/ship/reference/intent.yaml` | Ship intent | MODIFY — intent paragraph, add C7, F7, S3 |
| `core/components/plays/ship/SKILL.md` | Compiled ship | RE-BAKE via `/create-play --rebake ship` |
| `.claude/specs/idsd/idsd.md` | IDSD spec | MODIFY — P13 ✅ COMPLETE, add review-pr detail, note ship's bypass-conditional step |
| `core/components/agents/repo-orchestrator.md` | Agent | VERIFY / minor extend — git log + gh pr commands for reviewer selection |

## Technical Approach

**Strategy:**

1. **Taxonomy as a mapping layer, not a new rule set.** `pr-severity-taxonomy.md` enumerates existing KB standard IDs and assigns each to P1/P2/P3/P4 with a *mechanical* matching rule (keyword/path pattern + required evidence). No LLM judgment.
   `| Standard ID | Severity | Match Rule (grep/path glob) | Evidence Required |`

2. **Config precedent.** Add `review-pr:` block and `standards:` resolver in `core/config.yaml`:
   ```yaml
   standards:
     pr-severity-taxonomy: ./core/components/memory/standards/pr-severity-taxonomy.md
   review-pr:
     bypass: false
     confidence_threshold: 0.5
     reviewer_count: 3
     standards_order: [kb, ltm, stm]
     fallback_reviewers: []
     activity_window_days: 90
   ```

3. **New skill `quality-check-scoped` (not an extension).** Inputs: `diff_path`, `changed_paths[]`, `standards_set`, `severity_taxonomy_path`, `intent_summary`. Loads only KB files whose domain is touched by `changed_paths`, evaluates each relevant standard check against the diff as a single pass, classifies findings via the taxonomy, emits `findings.yaml` with `{standard_id, severity, file, line, evidence, taxonomy_rule_id}`. NEVER full-repo. Reuses KB data, not execution model.

4. **review-pr play, Structure A.** 3 domain agents + repo-orchestrator utility:
   - **Pre-flight (repo-orchestrator, utility):** resolve config, fetch PR diff/paths/body/linked-issue via `gh pr view` + `gh pr diff`, validate taxonomy file exists.
   - **Step 1 Context Load (tech-designer, domain):** resolve standards set from `standards_order`, bind intent from `spec/intent.yaml` or PR body; emit `context.yaml`.
   - **Step 2 Scoped Quality Eval (quality-auditor, domain):** invoke `quality-check-scoped`; emit `findings.yaml`.
   - **Step 3 Confidence & Routing (tech-designer, domain):** compute `confidence = 0.25*scan_coverage + 0.35*citation_rate + 0.20*intent_alignment + 0.20*precedent_match`. Emit `confidence.yaml` with routing decision: `block` if any P1; `escalate` if confidence < threshold; else `pass`.
   - **Step 4 Reviewer Selection (repo-orchestrator, domain for ranking):** `git log --since="{activity_window_days} days ago" --format='%ae' -- {changed_paths}` per path, exclude PR author, recency-weighted ranking (last 30d x3, 30-60d x2, 60-90d x1), take top `reviewer_count`, fall through to `fallback_reviewers` if <3.
   - **Step 5 Post & Route (repo-orchestrator, utility):** post structured PR comment; on P1 → `gh pr review --request-changes`; on P1 OR low confidence → `gh pr edit --add-reviewer`.
   - **Step 6 Evidence & Self-commit (repo-orchestrator, utility):** write STM evidence and self-commit.

   **Domain-agent count:** tech-designer (×2), quality-auditor, repo-orchestrator-as-ranker = 3 unique domain agents. Well under 5.

5. **Ship delta.** Change intent paragraph: *"Auto-proceeds end-to-end unless the `review-pr` quality gate blocks (configurable via `review-pr.bypass` in `core/config.yaml`)."* Add C7 (bypass-conditional gate), F7 (ship merged without gate when bypass=false), S3 (reviewer sees gate outcome). Compiled SKILL.md gains conditional Step 2.5.

6. **Sync deployment.** Batch `/sync-claude` at the end.

**Alternatives Considered:**
- Extend `/quality-check` with `--scope diff` flag → rejected. `/quality-check` launches 11 parallel subagents on full repo; bolting scoped mode forces dual execution models into one skill.
- Taxonomy authored as new rules from scratch → rejected. Existing 11-domain KB already has citable IDs. Duplicating would fragment standards.
- Reviewer selection inside `create-pr` → rejected per constraint. Review routing lives where review decisions live.
- Runtime config reading via intent-resolver → rejected. L2 has no runtime resolver; play pre-flight reads config directly (same pattern as `ship`).
- Ship reads review-pr.bypass inside sub-play vs orchestrator → chose orchestrator-level. Consistent with ship's existing C1/C2 pre-flight.

**Risks:**
| Risk | Mitigation | Severity |
|---|---|---|
| Severity taxonomy drifts into LLM-judged rules | Enforce mechanical form: every row has grep/path pattern + standard ID; skill rejects findings without both | High |
| `precedent_match` requires STM resolution-trace infra that may not exist | Define as "fraction of findings whose cited standard ID appears in prior STM evidence for this repo"; default 0.5 if empty | Medium |
| Ship's contract change ("no approvals at any stage") is user-visible | Update intent language; document bypass flag; call out in idsd.md | Medium |
| `git log` reviewer selection swamps dominant committers | Recency weighting + `fallback_reviewers` + author exclusion | Low |
| New paths have no git history → <3 reviewers | `fallback_reviewers` config; fail loud if empty and <3 | Medium |
| Chicken-and-egg: `/create-play` for review-pr before skills exist | Dependency order: taxonomy → config → skill → intent → bake | Medium |
| Intent-hash drift on ship after re-bake | `/create-play --rebake ship` recomputes hash; verification re-reads compiled intent_hash | Low |

## Open Decisions (resolved)

**(a) KB severity taxonomy authorship — derive from existing `/quality-check` domains.** The 11-domain KB already defines citable IDs with assessment checklists; the taxonomy is a *mapping* layer from those IDs to P1..P4 buckets with mechanical rules. Hand-authoring from scratch would fragment the standards corpus, violate "one source of truth," and force `quality-check-scoped` to maintain two parallel ID spaces.

**(b) Confidence math weights — weighted, not equal.** `confidence = 0.25*scan_coverage + 0.35*citation_rate + 0.20*intent_alignment + 0.20*precedent_match`. `citation_rate` (fraction of findings citing a real standard ID) is the strongest deterministic signal and directly enforces F3 — it deserves highest weight. Weights sum to 1.0. Documented in play intent so changes require a re-bake.
